tactical_shape: Started with sustained early pressure and a high defensive squeeze that kept Iraq pinned back
Built attacks down the right, with Koundé involved in the move for the opening goal
Recovered quickly after the stoppage and punished Iraq on transitions / simple finishes after defensive errors
standout_players: Kylian Mbappé: two goals, including a long-range opener and a second after the restart; dominant and central to the win
Ousmane Dembélé: scored the third, his first goal at a major tournament, and assisted Mbappé for the second
Michael Olise: assisted the first goal and later teed up Dembélé for the third
Jules Koundé: part of the right-sided buildup for the opener
fatigue_injuries: Dembélé and Michael Olise were withdrawn on the hour to preserve them for tougher matches
home_read.momentum: Qualified for the knockout rounds with six points and looked comfortably in control despite the extraordinary stoppage.

tactical_shape: Largely pinned deep, with the defensive line unable to move beyond the six-yard box during France’s first-half siege
Preferred to keep the ball and out-possess France rather than press aggressively
Showed brief threat after the restart through direct crossing, including a header from Ali al-Hamadi
standout_players: Ali al-Hamadi: replaced the injured captain and immediately threatened with a header just wide
Merchas Doski: delivered the cross for that early post-restart chance
fatigue_injuries: Captain Aymen Hussein was injured and replaced by Ali al-Hamadi
The two-hour stoppage was said by the coach to have made things harder and may have contributed to mistakes
away_read.momentum: Out-possessed France but were ultimately undone by poor defending and the restart disruption; left with the sense they competed but were outscored by a superior side.
